name: Reliability, Safety and Control
definition: To ensure that AI systems are safe and secure, and perform as intended.
verbatim: AI systems must operate reliably and consistently under both normal and unexpected conditions. Systems must provide proper access control and protection in critical situations. Providers must conduct rigorous testing and validation to confirm algorithms perform accurately. Systems must be resilient against unauthorized access and resist compromise by unauthorized parties. Includes the requirement for human control mechanisms, particularly in high-risk applications. Developers must validate AI systems before deployment through comprehensive protocols.
keyElements: Reliable and consistent operation under normal and unexpected conditions
Proper access control and protection in critical situations
Rigorous testing and validation of algorithm accuracy
Resilience against unauthorized access and compromise
Human control mechanisms for high-risk applications

stakeholderObligations.developers: Conduct rigorous testing, validate algorithms, implement access controls, deploy validation protocols
stakeholderObligations.endUsers: Report unexpected AI behaviour, maintain awareness of system limitations
stakeholderObligations.policymakers: Establish safety standards and testing requirements for AI systems
